default.notes: Conservative default for unknown stage classes.

alphafold.notes: AlphaFold needs A100-class GPU memory. Storage for MSA databases.

deepvariant.notes: DeepVariant is GPU-accelerated; CPU-only fallback is ~10x slower.

star-2pass.notes: STAR 2-pass needs the full genome index in memory.

aggregator.notes: I/O-bound on artifact concatenation; minimal compute.

alignment.description: Generic alignment (umbrella class — `alignment_quantification` is the production form for short-read RNA-seq; this row covers ATAC, ChIP, methylation, long-read)
alignment.notes: CPU-bound on minimap2 / bowtie2 / bwa-mem2; per-method overrides below adjust memory.

alignment_quantification.notes: STAR 2-pass needs 32 GB for human genome index alone. Memory-optimized family. Bump to 128 GB for large genomes.

analysis.description: Generic downstream-analysis stage (umbrella for class-less analytical steps)
analysis.notes: Conservative CPU shape; concrete classes (DE, clustering, etc.) have dedicated profiles.

batch_correction.description: Harmony / scVI / Combat — memory-intensive on cell-level data
batch_correction.notes: scVI uses GPU when present; Harmony/Combat are CPU-only.

benchmarking.notes: CPU-bound on hap.py / vcfeval; storage for truth-set + query VCF.

biological_interpretation.description: Narrative + LLM cross-check; mostly read-only on results
biological_interpretation.notes: No heavy compute; runs the claim_extractor + claim_verifier pipeline.

cell_communication.notes: CPU-bound R/Python; memory grows with cell count × ligand-receptor pair count.

clinical_trial.description: Umbrella class for clinical-trial taxonomy stages — mapped to trial_* sub-profiles when stage_class lands more specifically
clinical_trial.notes: CPU-bound; trial-specific sub-profiles (`trial_population_definition`, `trial_primary_endpoint`, `trial_safety_analysis`) override when matched.

clustering.description: Leiden/Louvain clustering, UMAP — memory for large cell counts
clustering.notes: scanpy/Seurat neighbor graph is O(n) memory. Scale for >500k cells.

data_acquisition.description: Download from repositories — network-bound, low compute
data_acquisition.notes: Network-bound. Burstable family preferred. Scale storage, not compute.

deconvolution.notes: CPU-bound; memory scales with reference single-cell atlas size. BayesPrism + CIBERSORTx have ≥32 GB recommendations.

differential_expression.description: DESeq2, edgeR, limma — single-threaded R, moderate memory
differential_expression.notes: Similar to normalization. DESeq2 fitType=parametric is memory-bound.

differential_transcript_usage.notes: CPU-bound; scales with isoform count × sample count.

dimensionality_reduction.notes: Memory scales with cell count × feature count; mostly CPU-bound.

forecast_modeling.description: ARIMA / Prophet / NeuralProphet / TimesNet model fit
forecast_modeling.notes: Per-series fits parallelize; deep models benefit from GPU.

gwas_summary_harmonization.notes: I/O-bound; pandas / DuckDB on tabular files.

harmonization.notes: CPU-bound; storage scales with cohort size. `metadata_harmonization` sub-profile covers the per-study metadata path.

integration.notes: Same as batch_correction shape but typically runs on integrated AnnData rather than per-sample.

interpretation.description: Biological interpretation umbrella (claim drafting, ontology lookup, narrative synthesis)
interpretation.notes: CPU-bound; ontology (GO/KEGG/Reactome) lookup tables + narrative drafting. Falls into `biological_interpretation` when matched specifically.

isoform_calling.notes: Long-read bound on alignment + read-cluster discovery.

locus_definition.description: GWAS locus definition (P+T clumping, FUMA / MAGMA-style window calls, cojo-slct)
locus_definition.notes: CPU-bound; scales with GWAS variant count. PLINK clumping is single-threaded.

metadata_harmonization.description: Per-sample metadata harmonization (canonical TSV with condition / age / grade)
metadata_harmonization.notes: CPU-bound; small data. Spec-required stage in single-cell + bulk taxonomies.

normalization.description: DESeq2 VST, TMM — single-threaded R, moderate memory
normalization.notes: R is single-threaded for most normalization. Memory matters more than cores.

pathway_enrichment.notes: I/O bound on database fetch; minimal compute.

peak_calling.notes: I/O-bound on BAM scan; CPU-bound on peak detection.

peptide_search.notes: Search-engine grunt work; CPU-bound, scales with thread count.

protein_quantification.notes: DIA-NN is GPU-aware for some steps; default CPU path.

quantification.description: Read → feature quantification (Salmon / kallisto / featureCounts / featureCounts-multi)
quantification.notes: CPU-bound; memory grows with transcriptome index size (Salmon ~12 GB on Ensembl human v115).

reporting.notes: CPU-bound; small data. Quarto / RMarkdown / MultiQC.

review.notes: Review tasks are human-assigned. No instance needed.

sensitivity_comparison.notes: CPU-bound; the scaffolding stage itself is light — the per-method runs use the underlying class's profile.

spatial_clustering.notes: Memory scales with spot/cell count × feature count.

spatially_variable_genes.notes: CPU-bound; memory scales with spot count × gene count.

taxonomic_classification.notes: Memory dominated by reference DB load (Kraken2 standard ≈ 50 GB).

tissue_prioritization.notes: CPU-bound; reference tissue panel sets memory floor.

trajectory.description: Pseudotime / trajectory inference (Monocle3, Slingshot, scFates, Chronocell)
trajectory.notes: CPU-bound; trajectory-graph construction scales with cell count. Pseudotime claims have no field-consensus ground truth (Round-2 §3.10).

trial_population_definition.notes: Pure pandas / arrow joins; CPU-bound, fast.

trial_primary_endpoint.description: Primary endpoint estimand evaluation (MMRM / CMH / KM)

trial_safety_analysis.notes: Tabulation + summary statistics; CPU-bound, small data.

variant_annotation.notes: Database fetch; offline-cached database is preferred.

variant_calling.description: GATK, bcftools, DeepVariant — compute + memory intensive
variant_calling.notes: GATK HaplotypeCaller is both CPU and memory hungry.

variant_filtering.description: VEP / SnpEff / hard-filter expressions on VCF
